statement: A flywheel (solid disk) has mass 10 kg and radius 0.5 m. It spins at 120 rpm. A braking torque of 8 N·m is applied. How long does it take to stop? How many revolutions does it make before stopping?
common_student_errors: Forgetting to convert RPM to rad/s: ω = 120·2π/60 = 4π rad/s
Using kinematics equations for linear motion (v = v0 + at) directly without the rotational analogues (ω = ω0 + αt)
Confusing angular displacement in radians with number of revolutions (must divide by 2π)
